REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (283 Ramsell Street, San Francisco, Ca 94132)
Off Calendar Jul-01-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

The sale was confirmed at $1,038,000 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). An additional bond in the amount of $826,000 is required.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: D. Johnson. Not reported.

Petition For Authority To Pay Former And Current Litigation Counsel; For Lien Against Real Property; Petition To Pay For Various Out Of Pocket Probate Expenses And For Lien Against Real Property; And Petition To Attribute All Fees And Costs Of Probate Administration And Final Litigation Fees As A Lien Against The Real Property
Off Calendar Mar-03-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances; Avid Alavi appeared by zoom for Scott Hodges (Administrator).

The Court asked if the lien against the real property is being requested in the event Mr. Gardner were to file an appeal and prevail in the form of a judgment. Ms. Alavi stated that the Court order dated January 22, 2020 authorizes the probate administration costs and litigation fees, that at the time when the petition was filed the trial had not happened, that the trial did happen and they are the prevailing party, that they have until February 22, 2021 for filing of an appeal from Mr. Gardner and that to date, nothing has been filed. Ms. Alavi also raised the issue of all of the vendors getting paid, stated that no one has been paid since 2016, that there have been significant out of pocket expenses and asked if the Court wants to continue this for two weeks to see if Mr. Gardner appeals. In addition, Ms. Alavi stated that if Mr. Gardner wins the law suit, the property would be in Mr. Gardner's name, that he could sell the property and not pay anyone, that they need a lien on the property to secure payment of fees, that the January 22. 2020 order does not provide for a lien and explained language in the order regarding the adverse possession claim. Furthermore, Ms. Alavi stated that notice was not given to Mr. Gardner, but notice was given to Mr. Vann, that Mr. Vann is non-responsive, that they have no idea where Mr. Gardner lives, that notice was sent to Ramsell Street and an address in Alabama, and that there was an indication that Mr. Gardner was in Lake County, but they have no idea where in Lake County he may live. The Court questions how it can order a lien on the property for unknown amounts. Ms. Alavi stated that this is being done to try and protect them, that it is being in an abundance of caution, that the amount of work and time they put into this was very significant and noted that fees would need to be approved by the Court. The Petition for Authority to Pay Former and Current Litigation Counsel' for Lien Against Real Property, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition For Authority To Pay Former And Current Litigation Counsel; For Lien Against Real Property; Petition To Pay For Various Out Of Pocket Probate Expenses And For Lien Against Real Property; And Petition To Attribute All Fees And Costs Of Probate Administration And Final Litigation Fees As A Lien Against The Real Property

Off Calendar Oct-21-2019 Advanced From Jan-06-20 To Dec-11-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order The Adverse Possession Claim Is Subject To The Expenses Of Probate Administration
Off Calendar Dec-11-2019 Continued To Jan-08-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Andrew Schultz for Scott Hodges (Personal Representative).

Mr. Schultz stated that he does not think the property has been sold, that there was a petition to list and sell the property, but it is in abeyance, that the house is vacant and that Mr. Gardner was evicted. The Court informed Mr. Schultz that the order needs to track Probate Code 9651 and that he needs to tell the Court if he is proceeding under (d)(1) or (d)(2). Mr. Schultz stated that he knows there is an adverse possession claim because it is in this Court. The Court stated that Mr. Schultz can file a request for judicial notice with a narrative, that it knows nothing about that proceeding and that it needs enough information to make a finding. Mr. Schultz stated that he needs to file a supplement and a request for judicial notice. The Petition for Order that Adverse Possession Claim is Subject to the Expenses of the Probate Administration is continued to January 8, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Loo. Not reported.

Petition For Authority To Pay Litigation Counsel And For Lien Against Real Property
Off Calendar Jan-16-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Avid Alavi for Scott Hodges.
Daniel Conrad litigation Counsel for Scott Hodges.

The Court expressed concern regarding the amount of fees requested exceeding the amount authorized in the order and parties not obeying Court orders. Mr. Conrad apologized to the Court, explained circumstances and what the services provided entailed (MSJ/trial/adverse possession claim/research/landlord tenant issue), the benefit provided to estate, spoke of time/hours spent, stated that only one Attorney billed for research, etc. Ms. Alavi explained that the only asset is the real property in San Francisco, that Mr. Conrad's services were essential and they otherwise would not have probate. The Petition for Authority to Pay Litigation Counsel, etc. is taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.
